body.title: Consequence check: recall-first tooling in skills
body.result: Observation: recall(X) calls wired into skills. Effect (1) confirmed: recall returns empty until T-librarian-retrieval-service ships (in this batch). No latency issues observed. Status: partial — will clear when retrieval service lands.
body.description:
**What changed:** Added mandatory recall(X) calls to sprint-ticket, context-load, and sorted skills — recall before bash as the default pattern.
**Predicted unintended effects:** (1) recall(X) returning empty results for every query until T-librarian-retrieval-service ships — the step exists but adds latency with no benefit; skills should gracefully handle empty/unavailable recall. (2) recall queries may be too broad (ticket title) and return noise rather than signal — making the step feel useless and encouraging bypass. (3) Skills calling recall before T-memory-write-path ships means querying a store that isn't being populated correctly yet — stale data could mislead.
**Signals to watch:** recall step latency in sprint-ticket logs; whether recall results actually influence the sprint plan (do we cite them?); recall returning useful vs empty results ratio.
**Gate condition:** After T-recall-in-skills ships.

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Test plan:** no tests — this is an observation ticket
